source_pattern: Pure functional with effects in IO monad

notes: Int is bounded, Integer is arbitrary precision

source_pattern: Web frameworks - Django/Flask → Servant/Yesod (see framework-specific guides)

notes: everything impure goes in IO monad
let Haskell deduce types where possible
replace duck typing with explicit constraints

target_pattern:
```haskell
import Control.DeepSeq (force)

-- Force strict evaluation when needed
processData :: [Int] -> [Int]
processData items = force results
  where
    results = map expensiveFunc items

-- Or use strict versions
import qualified Data.Map.Strict as M
```
notes:
```haskell
-- Python: Eager evaluation
def process_data(items):
    results = [expensive_func(x) for x in items]
    print(f"Processed {len(results)} items")
    return results

-- Haskell: Lazy evaluation (different behavior!)
processData :: [Int] -> [Int]
processData items = results
  where
    results = map expensiveFunc items  -- NOT evaluated yet!
    -- length results would force evaluation
```
